label: Squarish 2BR — public/private split, open-plan LDK (dining and kitchen side-by-side at rear)

adjacencies.note: ensuite's south wall is standard's north wall — solid (no door, ensuite is private to master)
common's south wall is standard's north wall — solid (door faces dining across the dividing wall)
shared plumbing wall between the two baths — single wet stack on the bath bar
ensuite's north wall is master's south wall — this is the ensuite door (only access to ensuite)
common's north wall is master's south wall — solid (no door, common is publicly accessed)
standard accessed across the dividing wall from living (both at front)
master accessed across the dividing wall from dining (both toward rear)
common bath's door faces dining across the dividing wall — public access
open-plan LDK: living flows into dining (orientation chosen by solver)
open-plan LDK: dining flows into kitchen (orientation chosen by solver)

setback_elements.note: side placement — keeps the rear elevation behind the kitchen open for windows/lanai instead of occupied by the dirty kitchen

notes: Open-plan variant of the split topology. Same public/private wall and same
private sleeping column (standard → bath bar → master), but the LDK is NOT
stacked front-to-rear. Living still touches the front exterior (solver hard
rule) and kitchen still touches the rear (solver hard rule), but dining is
free to land beside one of them rather than between them. In practice the
- Living spans the front of the public column.
- Dining and kitchen sit side-by-side at the rear of the public column.
Use this when the brief calls for a casual / modern feel — the cook is
integrated with the dining/living area rather than tucked at the back.
Use the stacked variant for traditional sala-comedor-kusina layouts.
Differs from squarish_two_bedroom_split_stacked.json only in the absence of
the [living, dining, kitchen] entry in front_to_rear_stacks.
